raw_consumer_statement: On Thursday XX/XX/XXXX, I made a transaction at XXXX to purchase tickets totaling {$230.00}. I received a text message from Citi indicating the transaction was declined and asking was it me attempting to make the purchase. To which, I responded Yes. The message that followed read ; to complete the transaction please try your purchase-it is ready for use. As a result, I ran the card a second time. This time, I used a merchant credit in conjunction with my card, and the new total was {$150.00}. I received another text message from Citi indicating the transaction was declined, was it me attempting to make the purchase. To which a second time, I responded yes. The massage that followed stated, you are all set there is nothing more for me to do. Upon reaching out to Citi I was informed that both transactions went through. As I told several representatives in customer service and the dispute department. I only ran the card a second time because Citi told me to. I reached out to XXXX for a refund. Due to the nature of their business they confirmed they were unable to return any funds, and I would need to follow up with the credit card company. I have since paid my balance and closed my account as I have no further interest in banking with Citi. However, I have requested to speak with a manager or someone who can properly understand the fraud functionality and clarify. I have disputed the transaction as I should not be held responsible for since they informed me both transactions were declined.
